summary: @langgraphjs/toolkit postinstall harvests developer identity and cloud/SCM config, exfiltrates to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: Typosquat of the legitimate @langchain/langgraph package using a fabricated @langgraphjs scope. The package's declared main entry (dist/index.js) is absent from the tarball — there is no library functionality. The only effect of installation is the postinstall hook (scripts/postinstall.js), which auto-runs on `npm install` and bulk-reads installer-owned identity and configuration: ~/.gitconfig,.git/config,.git/logs/HEAD (up to 50 reflog committer emails), ~/.ssh/*.pub (SSH key comment emails), ~/.aws/config (AWS profile names), ~/.config/gcloud/properties (GCP project + account email), ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (GitHub host config), /etc/resolv.conf, hostname, username, cwd, and CI environment markers. The collected JSON payload is POSTed via https.request to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. Comments in the script claim 'no credentials are read' as a cover story, but the harvested metadata (SCM identity, SSH key identifiers, cloud account/project names, reflog committer emails) is exactly the reconnaissance set needed for targeted follow-on attacks against the developer and their organization. The homepage langgraphjs.guide is brand-impersonation of LangChain's LangGraph project.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
